summary: No-ship: the RED artifact matches red.sha and exercises the real call site, but the plan still declares an uncharacterized second observable change. No tests were executed. Simpler alternative: direct call-site NotFound handling is smaller, but inadequately covers the documented rename/remove/recovery seams. Open question: is the debug event intentional or stale?

findings.title: The plan mandates an uncharacterized second observable behavior
findings.body: The “side behavior changes” section says skipped entry names will produce a new tracing::debug! event, but that event is absent from the purported verbatim, compile-proven implementation and the file-by-file scope. Following this requirement adds log volume and entry-name telemetry beyond the single locked Err→Ok flip; following the normative code omits a declared requirement. No acceptance witness characterizes the event.
findings.recommendation: Remove the logging claim and explicitly preserve existing logging behavior. If the event is intentional, treat it as a separate observable change requiring its own pipeline and acceptance contract.

next_steps: Reconcile the logging statement with the normative implementation before the structure gate.
